gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the dataset-prep ownership inversion for import cycles, CLI behavior, public API and star-import compatibility, and legacy caller regressions. It found no issues and manually probed both document and legacy module CLIs with PYTHONPATH=src.
scope: document and legacy package README migration notes
verification: python -m py_compile src/document_kv_cache/dataset_prep.py src/restaurant_kv_serving/dataset_prep.py tests/test_dataset_prep.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py

poetry run pytest tests/test_dataset_prep.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_benchmark_plan.py tests/test_benchmark_plan_executor.py tests/test_benchmark_runner.py tests/test_release_evidence.py tests/test_project_governance.py::test_legacy_restaurant_imports_in_tests_are_explicitly_scoped -q
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
what_changed: Moved the V1 dataset-preparation implementation into document_kv_cache.dataset_prep.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.dataset_prep into a compatibility wrapper over the document dataset-prep module.
Kept document_kv_cache.dataset_prep.__all__ curated while preserving the legacy wrapper's broader historical import surface.
Updated package READMEs and public-package tests to record the new implementation boundary.
why: The generalized package should own V1 dataset normalization directly because it is part of the public benchmark workflow for arbitrary documents. This is a behavior-preserving migration step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
